creator: dhawk@river.org
text:
My users like the 'pico' editor that's part of the pine mail port.
Control-T is supposed to run a spell checker but it complains about
/usr/bin/spell not being found. 

(I looked on 'best.com' and they don't have spell either.)

Fix: 

1. add spell to /usr/bin
2. alter the pine/pico port to use ispell if it exists

My preference would be to have spell just for backwards compatability
How-To-Repeat: If you've got pine/pico on your system then type  pico /tmp/trash
type in some stuff and then control-T
You have to use 'script' or an xterminal-log to find the error
message about /usr/bin/spell not found 

or do    ls /usr/bin/spell          ;-)
